channel.title: Special Variables on Nate Glenn's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Special Variables on Nate Glenn's Blog

item.title: When not to use Perl's Implicit close; Suffering from Buffering
item.author: garfieldnate <at> gmail <dot> com (Nathan Glenn)
item.description: This post is a quick note on a bug I had difficulty tracking down. One nice feature of Perl, introduced long before my time, is that of implicit closing. Perl closes filehandles for you when you forget (maybe on purpose). So the following is not a resource leak as a standalone script: 1 2 3 open my $file, '>utf8', '/path/to/new/file' or die "couldn't open file: $!"; print $file 'Hello!'; When the script finishes, Perl will close $file for you, so you can be nice and lazy.
